strengths: Current hands-on work at re:cinq building MCP-based agentic frameworks and AI-agent infrastructure provides rare production-adjacent exposure to the exact agentic tooling stack this CTO role demands.

Deep real-time distributed-systems pedigree: data-integration microservices at Dunnhumby handling millions of HTTP requests per second using async I/O, caching, and high-performance databases maps directly to real-time inference and streaming-data requirements.

Fintech domain exposure at RIDE Capital (investment software with strict TDD, trunk-based development, and CI/CD discipline) combined with a self-founded event-driven geospatial platform demonstrates both regulated-environment sensibility and end-to-end systems-design ownership.
concerns: Resume consistently positions Bogdan as a senior individual contributor; there is no evidence of managing, hiring, or scaling an engineering organization to the 10–30+ person threshold that is a hard requirement for this executive role.

Agentic AI work is described at the tooling and developer-framework level (MCP code generators, agentic workflow support); the role requires demonstrated architectural ownership of production multi-agent systems with direct financial or operational consequences—a higher bar.

No depth in core fintech infrastructure: credit-limit management, payment flows, counterparty risk modeling, or multi-jurisdiction compliance; RIDE Capital exposure was investment-software adjacent rather than fintech infrastructure proper.
response.justification: Bogdan brings a rare combination of current hands-on agentic-AI infrastructure work and a 16-year track record building high-throughput, event-driven, real-time distributed systems; his MCP framework contributions and agentic tooling experience at re:cinq place him closer to the technical frontier of this role than most candidates with equivalent backend depth, and his engineering-craft discipline—TDD, trunk-based development, CI/CD rigor across multiple employers—signals the production-reliability culture the company needs; the honest caveat is that his trajectory has been that of a highly accomplished individual contributor rather than an engineering executive, making this a high-ceiling bet on a technical leader who has not yet operated at the organizational or strategic scale the CTO seat demands.
